file_insights.changes: Refactored polling mechanism to event-driven filesystem watching
key_modifications: Moved done_dirs collection outside main loop for watcher setup
Spawned dedicated thread for filesystem watcher with 100ms debounce
Added needs_scan flag to ensure initial scan and event-triggered rescans
Implemented .bop extension filter for relevant filesystem events

patterns_discovered.description: Shifted from time-based polling (sleep in loop) to filesystem event notification with debounce
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: Used notify_debouncer_mini with 100ms debounce window to batch rapid filesystem changes

patterns_discovered.description: Spawned std::thread for watcher, uses std::sync::mpsc internally, bridges to async context via tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel
patterns_discovered.relevance: Necessary because notify crate requires synchronous channel receiver

patterns_discovered.description: Scans for both flat done/ and team-prefixed directories (team-*) for complete coverage
patterns_discovered.implementation_detail: Checks file_name().to_string_lossy() against team- prefix before adding to watch list

patterns_discovered.description: Only processes filesystem events where path.extension() == 'bop' and event kind is Any/AnyContinuous
patterns_discovered.rationale: Reduces false positives from unrelated directory changes

gotchas_discovered.description: Watcher runs in std::thread (blocking), not tokio::task (async). Requires std::sync::mpsc internally but bridges to tokio::sync::mpsc for async loop
gotchas_discovered.impact: Complexity in channel setup, potential confusion about threading model

gotchas_discovered.description: Parameter renamed to _poll_ms after removal of sleep call, but kept for backward API compatibility
gotchas_discovered.impact: Technical debt: API still accepts poll_ms but ignores it; should be removed in next cleanup

gotchas_discovered.description: Watcher only watches done_dir if done_dir.exists() at startup; new directories created later won't be watched
gotchas_discovered.impact: If new team-* directories created after merge_gate starts, their done/ subdirs won't be monitored until restart

gotchas_discovered.description: Event filter checks .bop extension; any .jobcard files without .bop extension won't trigger rescans
gotchas_discovered.impact: Depends on strict filesystem naming convention; any files not ending in .bop are silently ignored

gotchas_discovered.description: Set to true at start to ensure immediate initial scan, but subtle interaction with !once && !needs_scan branch
gotchas_discovered.impact: Once mode skips the recv().await, preventing early exit; behavior correct but requires careful reading

outcome_details.objective: Replace polling with filesystem watcher to reduce CPU usage and improve responsiveness
outcome_details.result: Achieved—moved from sleep-based polling to event-driven notification with 100ms debounce
outcome_details.commit: ff02dfe auto-claude: subtask-3-1 - Refactor run_merge_gate to use notify watcher with 100ms debounce

recommendations.rationale: Parameter is ignored after polling removal; cleanup for API clarity

recommendations.item: Watch for new team-* directories at runtime
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation only watches directories that exist at startup; dynamic team creation won't be monitored
recommendations.effort: Medium (requires periodic directory enumeration or inotify for parent dir)

recommendations.item: Add metrics for watcher events vs. actual processing
recommendations.rationale: Debouncing may filter events; visibility into event volume and filtering ratio helps validate design

recommendations.item: Consider using tokio::task instead of std::thread for watcher
recommendations.rationale: Current std::thread approach works but doesn't align with async-first pattern used elsewhere
recommendations.effort: Medium (requires async-compatible notify usage or wrapper)

recommendations.rationale: Async/sync boundary with two-stage channel (std + tokio) is non-obvious; inline comment or arch doc needed
